text.title: Team Methodology Overlay Mechanism Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the team-methodology-overlay-mechanism methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale. The terminal `skip-this-methodology` rule is the safety valve invoked by the decision tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Precedence chain MUST be: project-overlay > user-overlay > faion-base; no other order allowed.
Overlays MUST live in `.faion/overlays/project/` or `.faion/overlays/user/`; other paths ignored.
Whenever an overlay shadows a base methodology, a `conflict.log` entry MUST record overlay path + base path + timestamp.
rule.rationale: Silent shadowing causes confusion months later.

rule.statement: An overlay MUST conform to the same content/02-output-contract.xml schema as the base it replaces.
Each active overlay MUST be reviewed annually; un-reviewed → demoted to draft.
Author MUST stop and route elsewhere when the decision-tree resolves to a skip leaf; partial application of this methodology against an unfit input is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Forcing the methodology onto a mismatched input produces low-quality artefacts and erodes trust in the corpus.
